description: Develop through accumulated changes over an extended span. In biology, populations or lineages evolve across generations; an individual organism grows or adapts. Practices, cultures, and systems can also 'molei' when many changes gather over time.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced 'm' and let air pass through the nose into rounded middle-height back 'o'. Put the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for stressed 'l' and release into middle-height front 'e'. Keep the following high-front 'i' separate, without turning the final two vowels into a diphthong.
sound_symbolism: Voicing continues from the rounded nasal into the lateral, and the last two vowels unfold in separate syllables. The unhurried finish suits gradual development.
usage_notes: The verb is intransitive. 'thuroa' (grow) concerns increase or development in an organism or quantity, 'hinolu' (adapt) responds to conditions, and 'moluki' (transform) marks a more extensive change without requiring a long accumulation.

semantic_domains.community: gradual development of a culture or shared practice
semantic_domains.nature: change in populations or lineages across generations
semantic_domains.temporal: many changes accumulating over an extended span
